frameworks.name: Continuous Performance Test paradigm
frameworks.one_line: Sustained-attention task family used for ADHD detection.

frameworks.name: signal-detection / classification accuracy
frameworks.one_line: ADHD vs control discrimination via test scores.

core_question: Does the Mathematics CPT compare favorably with CPT II and the Brown Scale in detecting ADHD?
key_assumptions: CPT-type tasks index attention deficits relevant to ADHD
Self-report (Brown Scale) provides a partly convergent attention measure
manipulations: Test type: MATH-CPT vs CPT II vs Brown Scale
key_findings: MATH-CPT classified 74.5% of participants vs 71.6% for CPT II
Moderate correlations between the two CPT-type tests
MATH-CPT final attention formula correlated significantly with Brown Scale; CPT II did not
interpretations: MATH-CPT performs at least as well as CPT II for ADHD detection and aligns better with questionnaire-based attention ratings
